overview: The Marathon County Environmental Resources Committee approved two rezoning petitions unanimously and voted to forward a modified fee waiver resolution to the county board that will only waive reconnect fees for properties impacted by the April 17, 2026 tornado. The committee also received educational presentations from Solid Waste, Parks Recreation and Forestry, and Conservation Planning and Zoning departments.

agenda.item: Call to order and Pledge of Allegiance
Public hearing - Tim Veland rezoning petition (urban residential/general agriculture to rural residential, Town of Green Valley)
Public hearing - Jeffrey and Cynthia Schmidtz rezoning petition (Conservancy Recreation to General Agriculture, Town of McMillan)
Consideration of fee waiver for properties impacted by April 17, 2026 severe weather
Educational presentation - Solid Waste Department overview
Educational presentation - Parks, Recreation and Forestry Department overview
Educational presentation - Conservation Planning and Zoning Department overview

discussions.body: Supervisor Sefeld made a motion to approve the minutes, seconded by Supervisor Drebeck. The motion carried unanimously with no discussion.

discussions.item: Tim Veland rezoning petition - Town of Green Valley
discussions.body: Garrett Pogle presented the staff report for rezoning from urban residential and general agriculture to rural residential for a land division. The Town of Green Valley recommended approval on March 10, 2026. No public testimony was offered. Supervisor Drebeck moved to approve based on findings of fact and town approval, seconded by Supervisor Kurt. Motion carried unanimously.

discussions.item: Jeffrey and Cynthia Schmidtz rezoning petition - Town of McMillan
discussions.body: Staff presented the petition to rezone from Conservancy Recreation to General Agriculture. Supervisor Leur asked about the conservancy nature, and Garrett Pogle explained the property has a home and horse riding stable that are non-conforming uses in conservancy, and rezoning would make them conforming. He confirmed no natural area would be lost. Town of McMillan recommended approval on April 20, 2026. Supervisor Sefeld moved to approve, seconded by Supervisor Drew. Motion carried unanimously.

Director Lori Mskimmons presented a proposal to waive county zoning and private on-site wastewater treatment system fees for properties impacted by the April 17, 2026 severe weather. Supervisor Drew questioned where to draw the line for future events. Supervisor Leur asked about tracking costs. Supervisor Drebeck expressed concerns about fairness to others not in declared disaster areas and questioned consistency with recent fee increases. Supervisor Sefeld moved to forward the resolution, seconded by Supervisor Leur. Supervisor Drebeck then moved to amend the resolution to only waive reconnect fees ($160) and no other pouts or zoning fees, seconded by Supervisor Kurt. The amendment passed. The amended motion to forward to county board passed but was not unanimous.

actionItems: Forward amended fee waiver resolution to county board - waiving only reconnect fees ($160) for tornado-impacted properties
Rezoning for James Offer and David Tract property forwarded to county board for approval
Rezoning for Jeffrey and Cynthia Schmidtz property forwarded to county board for approval
Staff to track costs and requests related to fee waivers for future policy development
title: Marathon County Environmental Resources Committee Meeting
